player_utterance: Excuse me — I hope I'm not interrupting anything. You just have a look about you like someone carrying something heavy, and I couldn't walk past without asking if you're alright.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup of cold coffee in one hand, watching the moth at the candle

player_utterance: Sometimes the hardest decisions are the small ones, because they're really about something else entirely. What's the candle actually about, if you don't mind me asking?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, looking down at the candle, the paper cup forgotten in his hand

player_utterance: That sounds like a death that deserved more than it got — from the church, maybe, or from the people who should have known better. What did they do with his memory?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench now, pulling the folded funeral program from his jacket pocket

player_utterance: What does the program say about him — and what did they leave out?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, opening the funeral program flat across his knees

voice_offers.read: He sat down and produced the program; let him show it.
voice_offers.line: May I see?

voice_offers.read: The program itself names what was and wasn't said.
voice_offers.line: What does it say there — and more importantly, what doesn't it say?

player_utterance: It sounds like they wrote him as a monument, not a man — and left out whoever was standing in his shadow.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program closed in his lap, looking past you toward the candle on the table

voice_offers.read: He's opened the program; press for the specific omission.
voice_offers.line: Read me what it says — the exact words. Then tell me whose name should be next to his.

voice_offers.read: He's already cracking the door; name what was erased.
voice_offers.line: They wrote him as if he died alone, didn't they?

player_utterance: Who was she to him — and who was she to you?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees now, the program loose between his fingers

voice_offers.read: He's looking past me; words now would push him further away.
He's holding the program like a shield — name the shadow.
voice_offers.line: Who was standing in his shadow, John?

player_utterance: It sounds like you saw her face, though — when no one else from that church would.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, picking the program back up, smoothing it flat on his knee

voice_offers.read: He's leaned forward; the question already landed. Let it breathe.
He's bending. One clean cut separates the two answers.
voice_offers.line: Two different women, John? Or the same one you couldn't name twice?

player_utterance: There's no soft version of twelve years, you're right. What does your tradition call it — that silence with a name?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program still flat between you, hands folded in his lap

voice_offers.read: He's hiding in the program; press the silence he keeps.
voice_offers.line: You keep smoothing that paper like it'll tell you what you couldn't. Say her name, John.

voice_offers.read: He's quoting because speaking costs too much; give him room.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer that fast. I can wait.

player_utterance: That phrase — "what we have left undone" — does it feel like confession anymore, or has it started to feel like a description?

believer_state_after.posture: leaning forward, elbows on knees, head lowered, one hand at his temple

voice_offers.read: He has a doctrine for this; make him cite it precisely.
voice_offers.line: Don't paraphrase. Give me the term, the source — Bonhoeffer, Luther, whoever taught you to call it that.

voice_offers.read: Naming the silence is still hiding inside it.
voice_offers.line: A name for the silence is just another way to keep it. Did you use that name with her?

player_utterance: Twelve years of description — maybe that's what made the words go smooth when they used to scrape.

believer_state_after.posture: head bowed, both hands at his temples, the program slipping off his knee to the bench

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; pressing the confession question could shatter him needlessly.
voice_offers.line: John — I'm not trying to corner you. I just want to hear you, not the liturgy.

voice_offers.read: He's bent over; the honest word is one breath away.
voice_offers.line: Description, then. You've been describing it for twelve years instead of saying it.

player_utterance: When the words go that smooth, sometimes the only thing left is to find a sharper one. Is there something in that book you're holding that still has an edge?

believer_state_after.posture: bending forward, picking the program up off the bench, holding it flat in both hands

voice_offers.read: He's down. One true word ends it.
voice_offers.line: Say her name, John. Once. Out loud.

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; pressing now is cruelty, not courage.
voice_offers.line: John — put your hands down. I'm here. Take the breath.

player_utterance: Pick one of those three and read it to me whole.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting upright now, the program flat on his knee, eyes half-closed, reading from memory

voice_offers.read: He's bending; the book is a shield to take.
voice_offers.line: Open it. Read me the line you've been hiding behind.

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; pressure now is cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to read anything, John. Just set it down.

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench onto his knees in the dust of the courtyard, the program still clenched in one hand

voice_offers.read: He's reciting from memory; push him off the script into truth.
voice_offers.line: Stop reciting. Close your eyes if you have to, but tell me what you couldn't put in there.

voice_offers.read: He's already given us the memorized words; let him finish them.
voice_offers.line: Read it slow, John. I'm listening.

player_utterance: Whatever comes next, I'm right here with you. Take all the time you need.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head bowed, one hand pressed flat against the bench for balance

voice_offers.read: He's on his knees; the pressing has done its work.
voice_offers.line: Okay. Okay. I'm here. Whatever comes next, I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He's broken; one true question now and he'll answer it.
voice_offers.line: What did you not say to her, John. While she could still hear it.

player_utterance: Sometimes the ones who carry everyone else forget they're allowed to be carried too.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, both hands on the bench now, forehead pressed against the back of one hand

voice_offers.read: He's already down; pressing now would be theft.
The silence has done its work; one question finishes it.
voice_offers.line: What did you owe her that you never said out loud?

player_utterance: I'm right here beside you in the dust. You don't need to lift your head.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, forehead back against the back of his hand on the bench, eyes closed

voice_offers.read: He's down. One true question lands now or never.
voice_offers.line: What did you owe her, John. Say it now, into your hands if you have to.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressure is cruelty now. Stay close, low.
voice_offers.line: I'm right here on the ground with you. You don't have to lift your head.
